code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent sentence.
    """
    # Simple mapping from predicate to a readable phrase.
    # Refined verb mapping for more natural phrasing
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to a height of",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
    }

    def _clean(text: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        return text.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        verb_phrase = verb_map.get(triple.predicate, "has")
        obj = _clean(triple.object)

        # Special handling for count‑type predicates
        if triple.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
            unit = "rooms" if triple.predicate == "numberOfRooms" else "beds" if triple.predicate == "bedCount" else "floors"
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {verb_phrase} {obj} {unit}"
        elif triple.predicate == "language":
            # e.g., Denmark | language | Faroese language → "The Faroese language is spoken in Denmark."
            clause = f"The {obj} is spoken in {triple.subject}"
        elif triple.predicate == "architect":
            # e.g., Adare Manor | architect | James Pain → "The architect of Adare Manor was James Pain."
            clause = f"The architect of {triple.subject} was {obj}"
        elif triple.predicate == "leader":
            # e.g., India | leader | Sumitra Mahajan → "The leader of India is Sumitra Mahajan."
            clause = f"The leader of {triple.subject} is {obj}"
        elif triple.predicate == "leaderTitle":
            clause = f"{triple.subject}'s leader title is {obj}"
        elif triple.predicate == "chancellor":
            clause = f"{obj} is the chancellor of {triple.subject}"
        elif triple.predicate == "keyPerson":
            clause = f"{obj} is a key person of {triple.subject}"
        elif triple.predicate == "capital":
            clause = f"{obj} is the capital of {triple.subject}"
        else:
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {verb_phrase} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause.rstrip())

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
